issue.body:
ADR-0005 §2. The reviewer emits a `rule-drift` finding with ready-to-paste rule text when a PR introduces an undocumented pattern or makes an existing rule stale. A finding a human acts on — **no auto-write**.

**Anti-noise (ADR §2):** propose only if **no already-loaded Tier-A/B rule covers the pattern**; **≤1 rule-drift finding per persona-pass per PR**; dedup via `src/output/aggregate.ts` + `clearPriorInline` (no re-propose on re-review).

**Ship gate (council, measurement-first):** a **paired fixture** (mirror `scripts/measure-rules.ts`) with trigger cases (should propose) AND non-trigger/already-covered cases (should stay silent). Metric = **proposal precision** at ≥3 runs, reported as a range: proposes on triggers most runs, ~zero false proposals on clean/covered. Not "proposed something plausible once."

Blocked-by (council priority): recall (#45) + honest model interval (#49) come first — §2 assumes a reviewer worth tuning.
issue.title: M6 §2: rule-drift finding (propose a paste-ready rule)

pr.body:
## What

Implements **ADR-0005 §2 — rule-drift emission** (issue #70): the reviewer proposes **one** ready-to-paste `.review-rules` block when a PR introduces an undocumented recurring pattern or makes an existing rule stale. The render side already shipped in #75 (`📖` marker + paste block); this wires the **emission** half through the two-pass worker.

This is the "improve over time" mechanism from ADR-0005 — a *finding a human chooses to act on*, never an auto-write.

## How

- **`WorkerRequest.proposeRuleDrift`** gates the whole thing. `src/assembly/review.ts` sets it `true` **only when the repo has adopted the rules/docs system** (`preamble.length > 0` — Tier-A `.review-rules` and/or Tier-B `contextDocs` were loaded). Repos that opted out see zero behavioral change — it's a pure no-op when the flag is false/undefined.
- **`RULE_DRIFT_NOTE`** is appended to the analysis system prompt only when enabled. It encodes the §2 trigger (recurring / not-already-covered) plus a concrete paste format so a conservative model actually emits a usable block.
- **Schema + structurer**: `submit_findings` gains an optional `proposedRule`; `STRUCTURER_SYSTEM` tells the extractor to copy a proposed block verbatim onto the most relevant finding (never invent one).
- **`capRuleDrift`** (pure, exported) enforces the §2 anti-noise cap **deterministically**: at most **one** proposal per pass, kept on the first finding — a disobedient model can't spam proposals. `submittedToFinding` extracted + exported alongside it.

## Anti-noise discipline (from §2)

- ≤1 proposal per pass — enforced in code (`capRuleDrift`), not just prompt-hope.
- "Only if no already-loaded rule covers it" — instructed to the model, which sees the loaded rules in its prompt.
- Same dedup-on-repost as any finding (unchanged — `aggregate.ts` / `clearPriorInline`).

## Direction-agnostic

The emission wiring is needed whether a proposed rule is later **human-pasted** (shipped, #75) or **auto-written** (the maintainer's stated preference — still an open design debate). This PR does **not** pre-commit that question. One decision left for you: I gated on "rules adopted" rather than adding a config off-switch — flag if you'd rather it be explicitly `config`-controlled (e.g. default-off, opt-in) given the precision sensitivity below.

## Review round (sqw-reviewer, adversarial)

Ran `sqw-reviewer` before opening this — it returned REQUEST-CHANGES with 3 legit must-fixes, all addressed in the second commit:

1. **Pass 2 wasn't gated** (only Pass 1 was), so "pure no-op when off" didn't structurally hold — the structurer prompt + schema always advertised `proposedRule`, so a stray fenced block in analysis prose could be extracted on an opted-out repo. Now both passes gate symmetrically (`buildStructurerSystem` / `buildFindingsSchema` omit the sentence *and* the schema field when off). Unit-tested.
2. **`aggregateFindings` dropped `proposedRule`** when a rule-drift finding collapsed into a plain same-issue finding that landed first — the cap emits 1, but dedup could silently discard it before render. Merge now carries it over (new `aggregate.test.ts`; that file had zero coverage).
3. **The gate itself was untested** — added `proposeRuleDrift` assertions to the 3 existing `review.test.ts` capture tests.

Plus nits: trim before storing; per-pass-accurate prompt wording.

## Verification

- `verify:pr` green — **156 tests** (10 new across `worker-ruledrift.test.ts` + `aggregate.test.ts` + `review.test.ts`), typecheck + lint clean.
- **Real end-to-end smoke on the shipped free default** (glm-5.2 analysis + glm-5-turbo structurer), synthetic PR with a recurring `console.log(req.body)` pattern across two files + one loaded rule that does *not* cover it:
  - `proposeRuleDrift: true` → **1** well-scoped proposal (`description:` + `globs: src/api/**/*.ts`, correctly not restating the loaded rule), cap held.
  - `proposeRuleDrift: false` (control) → **0**.
  - Whether the model proposes on a given run varies — by design (at-most-one, only-when-it-clearly-qualifies).

## Related precision note

Separately measured this session (see `eval/RESULTS.md`): the deepseek structurer is ~3–4× noisier on clean cases than the free glm-5-turbo default. Rule-drift adds at most one finding per pass and is gated, so it doesn't move that needle — but it's why I kept the cap deterministic rather than trusting the prompt. #73 (precision cost of rules) is the place to measure drift's own FP rate once it's dogfooded.

Closes #70.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

https://claude.ai/code/session_018zaS3LENwZuc3785pA2zTp
pr.title: feat(worker): rule-drift emission (ADR-0005 §2, #70)
